Who I Am

Educator. Innovator. Thinker.

I am a Physics and Chemistry teacher at senior school level in Kenya, working across both the KCSE and IGCSE/Cambridge curricula. My teaching philosophy is built on one conviction: students should leave every lesson with sharper thinking, not just memorised content.

Outside the classroom, I serve as a trainer and judge at the Kenya Science and Engineering Fair (KSEF) — helping young scientists develop research rigour, intellectual integrity, and the confidence to present original work at a national level. It is among the most meaningful work I do.

"Science is not a collection of facts to be stored — it is a disciplined way of reasoning about the world. That is what I teach, in every lesson, for every curriculum."

I am a competitive chess player and certified trainer, drawn to the game for its demand for strategic depth and patience — qualities that also define great scientific thinking. My intellectual interests reach into nanotechnology and material science, where I follow emerging research on nanoscale phenomena and advanced materials with genuine fascination.

Whether working with a KCSE candidate in Nairobi or an IGCSE student preparing for Cambridge, my approach is the same: rigorous, honest, and student-centred.

🔬

Nanotechnology

Fascinated by matter at the nanoscale — where quantum effects reshape material behaviour.

🧱

Material Science

How atomic structure determines macroscopic properties — from graphene to smart biomaterials.

♟

Chess

Competitive player and trainer. Chess builds the same strategic patience that science demands.

🏆

KSEF

Training and judging young scientists at Kenya's premier science and engineering fair for 2+ years.

Expertise

Curricula I Teach

Deep, exam-aligned expertise across two major international science curricula — every student prepared to the precise standard their examining body demands.

🇰🇪KCSE · Kenya

Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education

Full coverage of the KNEC Physics and Chemistry syllabi for Form 1–4. Students are prepared for Paper 1, Paper 2, and — for Chemistry — the rigorous Paper 3 practical examination.

Physics P1 & P2Chemistry P1, P2 & P3 KNEC FormatMarking Schemes Mock Exams
Exam technique aligned to KNEC examiner expectations
Authentic past paper question styles in all materials
Chemistry practical write-up training for Paper 3
Form 1–4 full syllabus coverage
🌍IGCSE · Cambridge

Cambridge IGCSE & International Science

Teaching Physics and Chemistry to Cambridge IGCSE and international school students. Rigorous, inquiry-based instruction aligned to Cambridge Assessment International Education standards.

IGCSE Physics (0625)IGCSE Chemistry (0620) Cambridge StandardInternational Schools A-Level Foundation
Cambridge mark scheme and command word mastery
Practical and alternative-to-practical preparation
Extended and Core tier coverage
Suitable for international and expatriate students
What I Offer

Teaching Services

Flexible, professional science tutoring services tailored to every student's curriculum, schedule, and learning goals.

KCSE · IGCSE 👤

One-on-One Tutoring

Personalised sessions focused entirely on the individual student's needs — gaps identified, concepts rebuilt, exam confidence developed at their pace.

KCSE · IGCSE 👥

Group Tutoring

Small group sessions (2–6 students) that combine focused instruction with peer discussion — effective for schools and study groups seeking structured support.

Flexible 💻

Online Sessions

Live online tutoring via video call — accessible from anywhere in Kenya or internationally. Ideal for IGCSE students at international schools or students in remote areas.

Exam Prep 📝

Crash Courses

Intensive exam preparation programmes in the weeks before KCSE or IGCSE exams — covering high-yield topics, past paper technique, and mark scheme mastery under timed conditions.

Digital 📦

Study Materials & Notes

Purpose-built digital study packs — revision notes, mock papers, marking schemes, and practical guides — available for KCSE and IGCSE Physics and Chemistry.

Schools 🏫

School Partnerships

Curriculum support, staff development, exam material creation, and KSEF mentorship programmes for schools seeking to strengthen their science departments.

💬

Flexible Pricing — Contact for a Quote

Rates are tailored to the type of service, curriculum, frequency, and location. Reach out via email or WhatsApp for a personalised quote — all enquiries are welcome.

Beyond the Classroom

KSEF & Chess

Two disciplines outside formal teaching where I invest significant expertise — both grounded in the same love of disciplined, deep thinking.

2+ Years Experience

Kenya Science & Engineering Fair

I serve as both a trainer and judge at KSEF — Kenya's premier national platform for student scientific research. My role spans mentoring students through the full research cycle to evaluating projects against rigorous national and international standards.

Judging student science and engineering projects at national level
Training students in scientific methodology, data analysis, and presentation
Supporting schools in developing inquiry-based learning culture
Bridging the gap between classroom science and authentic research
Mentoring students for ISEF-affiliated international competition readiness
Player & Certified Trainer

Chess

Chess is more than a game — it is a training ground for the analytical mind. As both a competitive player and a trainer, I use chess to develop strategic thinking, patience under pressure, and focused problem-solving — skills that transfer directly to science and academic performance.

Experienced competitive chess player with structured training background
Training students and adults from beginner through intermediate level
Chess coaching for schools as a cognitive development programme
Connecting chess strategy patterns to scientific problem-solving habits
Available for school chess club setup and coaching contracts
Philosophy

How I Teach

The goal is never merely to pass an exam — it is to produce students who understand science and can use it to reason about the world.

01 —

Concepts Before Formulas

Every equation is the last step. Understanding is built first — layer by layer — before a formula is introduced. Students who understand always outperform students who memorise.

02 —

Exam Intelligence

KCSE and IGCSE have specific languages. Students learn to read command words, allocate marks strategically, and structure answers the way examiners reward — in both curricula.

03 —

Scientific Curiosity

Lessons connect to frontier science — nanotechnology, material science, clean energy — so students see where their learning leads beyond the syllabus.

04 —

Rigorous Practice

Structured revision, KNEC and Cambridge-format mock papers, and quality feedback. Confidence is earned through genuine, well-reviewed repetition — not reassurance.

05 —

Open Inquiry

Questions are always welcome. Science advances by challenging assumptions — that spirit is present in every lesson. Students learn to question, test, and think for themselves.

06 —

Whole-Student Development

Chess training, KSEF mentorship, and science teaching all serve one goal: producing thinkers who reason with clarity, work with integrity, and approach problems with confidence.
